title: Never conflate loading, not-found, and empty states
tip: When building data-dependent UI, always distinguish Loading (data fetch in progress), Not Found (fetch completed, resource does not exist or user lacks access), and Empty (fetch completed, resource exists but has no items). Conflating these causes Trip Not Found regressions during auth hydration.
avoid_when: static pages with no data dependencies
subtasks: check loading state handling
verify error vs empty distinction
test auth hydration timing
signals: flash of error state on page load
Trip Not Found shown briefly then resolves
empty state shown while data is loading
evidence: Documented as zero-tolerance path in CLAUDE.md. Recurring regression pattern in Chravel.

title: Trace field names end-to-end before patching data bugs
tip: When a data value renders incorrectly, trace the field from DB schema to Supabase types to hook/query to component props to render. Fix at the source layer, not via mapping hacks. Field name mismatches between layers are a historically common Chravel regression.
avoid_when: pure UI/styling bugs with no data dependency
subtasks: check DB column name
check generated types
check hook return shape
check component prop types
check render usage
signals: undefined field in component
type error on data access
stale or wrong value displayed
evidence: Documented in AGENTS.md section 5.2 as a stop-the-line bug class.

title: Trip Not Found flash during auth hydration
tip: When users see a brief Trip Not Found flash on valid trip URLs, the root cause is almost always the trip data fetch firing before auth session hydration completes. Gate data fetches on auth being fully resolved, not just present.
subtasks: reproduce with hard refresh
log auth state at query time
gate fetch on session resolved
verify no loading delay regression
signals: brief error flash then correct content
query returns null before auth resolves
Trip Not Found component mounts then unmounts
evidence: Zero-tolerance path in CLAUDE.md. Historical recurring regression.

title: Treat imports and sync as state machines, not parser calls
tip: For external data ingestion, define explicit run states (received/parsed/deduped/staged/confirmed/materialized) with idempotency keys and partial terminal states. UI toasts alone are insufficient because retries, replayed events, and parser ambiguity can create silent corruption.
subtasks: persist run + step records
attach external IDs + provenance
surface partial counts in UX
add replay/idempotency tests
signals: success toast but missing objects
duplicate imported records
cannot explain object origin
evidence: March 2026 integrations/import-export audit found fragmented semantics across gmail-import-worker, file-ai-parser, and export pipelines.

title: Gate all data fetches on fully hydrated auth session
tip: Supabase Auth returns null during session hydration, which is indistinguishable from logged out. All data fetches on auth-gated routes must wait for session hydration to complete (not just check session !== null). The useAuth hook tracks hydration state; use its isLoading/isReady signal before enabling queries.
applies_when: any TanStack Query with auth dependency
subtasks: verify query enabled flag gates on auth resolved
check useAuth returns hydration state
test with hard refresh on auth-gated route
confirm no flash of error during hydration
signals: data fetch returns 401 on page load then succeeds
Trip Not Found flash on valid URLs
query fires with null session
evidence: Zero-tolerance path in CLAUDE.md. useAuth.tsx is the single auth provider (46KB). Historical recurring regression documented in DEBUG_PATTERNS.md.

title: Enforce payment SDK boundary: RevenueCat for iOS, Stripe for web
tip: RevenueCat handles iOS/Android subscriptions; Stripe handles web checkout and webhooks. Never mix client SDKs across platforms. The subscription source of truth is the user_subscriptions table populated by webhooks, not client-side SDK state. Gate premium features on DB subscription status queried via hooks, not on RevenueCat/Stripe client cache which can drift.
subtasks: verify SDK platform detection in revenuecatClient.ts
check entitlement queries go through DB not SDK cache
verify webhook populates user_subscriptions
test paywall renders correctly per platform
signals: subscription shows as active on one platform but not another
entitlement check passes client-side but fails server-side
RevenueCat SDK error on web
evidence: RevenueCat integration in src/integrations/revenuecat/revenuecatClient.ts with platform detection. Stripe edge functions for web checkout. LESSONS.md SDK boot compatibility entry.

title: AI concierge writes go through pending actions buffer
tip: AI-initiated writes to shared trip state (tasks, polls, calendar events, places) must go through the trip_pending_actions buffer, not direct inserts. The user confirms or rejects each pending action. Use tool_call_id as an idempotency key to prevent duplicate actions from retries. Read-only operations (search, recommendations, lookups) bypass the buffer.
applies_when: any AI-initiated mutation to shared trip data
avoid_when: AI responses that only display information
subtasks: verify tool writes target pending_actions table
check idempotency key on tool_call_id
verify confirm/reject UI flow
test retry does not create duplicate action
signals: AI creates duplicate trip items
AI writes appear without user confirmation
tool call creates data in wrong table
evidence: AUDIT_CONCIERGE_LIVE.md documents tool call safety. AI concierge tools and guardrails doc in docs/AI_CONCIERGE_TOOLS_AND_GUARDRAILS.md.

title: Permission model varies by trip type: consumer open, pro role-based, event organizer-only
tip: Consumer trips allow all members to mutate. Pro trips use role-based permissions (admin/member/viewer). Event trips are organizer-only for mutations. Use useMutationPermissions(tripId) which resolves trip type once and returns flat boolean flags. This is client-side UX gating only; RLS policies are the authoritative enforcement layer. Never trust client-side permission checks for security.
applies_when: any mutation hook serving multiple trip types
avoid_when: features that only apply to one trip type
subtasks: check useMutationPermissions hook usage
verify RLS policy matches client permission logic
test mutation access across all three trip types
confirm unauthorized mutations are blocked server-side
signals: mutation succeeds in UI but fails at DB level
wrong permission level for trip type
consumer trip restricting member edits
evidence: docs/ACTIVE/SHARED_MUTATION_AUDIT.md documents the unified permission hook applied to 5 mutation hooks. LESSONS.md unified permission guard entry.

title: Calendar sync uses sync tokens and etags for idempotent bi-directional sync
tip: Google Calendar sync is bi-directional. Use sync_token for incremental sync and etag for conflict detection. Never overwrite remote events without comparing timestamps. The calendar-sync edge function is the authority for sync operations; client-side calendarService.ts reads/writes through Supabase, not directly to Google. Expired sync tokens require a full re-sync.
avoid_when: local-only calendar views with no sync
subtasks: verify sync token usage in edge function
check etag comparison on writes
test expired sync token fallback
verify timezone handling across boundaries
signals: duplicate calendar events after sync
event edits not propagating to Google
sync errors on token expiry
evidence: supabase/functions/calendar-sync/ edge function. docs/CALENDAR_ARCHITECTURE_REVIEW.md. calendarService.ts handles client-side queries.

title: Notification dual-path dedup prevents duplicate delivery
tip: Notifications can be generated by both database triggers and edge functions. Without deduplication, broadcasts and other multi-recipient events can fire duplicate push/email/in-app notifications. The notification system uses centralized dispatch with preference gating, quiet hours, and rate limiting. When adding new notification-generating features, use exactly one generation path per event type.
applies_when: any feature that sends notifications to multiple users
avoid_when: features that consume but don't generate notifications
subtasks: verify single generation path per event
check dedup mechanism
test quiet hours enforcement
verify rate limiting on high-fanout events
signals: duplicate notifications for same event
notifications ignoring quiet hours
notification storm from broadcast
evidence: NOTIFICATION_AUDIT.md documents dual-path dedup fix for broadcasts. Pattern applies to all new notification-generating features.

title: Gemini Live voice sessions require explicit lifecycle cleanup
tip: Gemini Live voice sessions use WebSocket connections with audio streaming state. Sessions must be explicitly closed on component unmount, navigation, and error. Audio state (microphone, playback) can get stuck if cleanup is skipped. The useGeminiLive hook manages session lifecycle; always use its cleanup functions rather than managing WebSocket directly. Voice sessions are separate from text concierge and dictation features.
applies_when: component unmount with active voice session
subtasks: verify cleanup on unmount
check audio state reset on session end
test navigation during active session
verify error handling closes session cleanly
signals: microphone stays active after leaving page
audio playback continues after session end
WebSocket connection leak
stuck voice UI state
evidence: AUDIT_CONCIERGE_LIVE.md found dead code in VoiceLiveOverlay and reconnect state issues. useGeminiLive.ts (54KB) manages full session lifecycle.

title: Media uploads use compression pipeline before storage
tip: Media uploads go through a client-side compression pipeline before being sent to Supabase Storage. Large images are resized and compressed; videos may have size limits. The mediaService handles upload orchestration while the image-upload edge function handles server-side processing. AI tagging via Gemini is async and failures are silently tolerated (non-blocking). Storage bucket policies enforce access control via RLS.
subtasks: verify compression runs before upload
check storage bucket RLS policies
verify AI tagging failure is non-blocking
test large file handling
signals: upload timeout on large files
storage quota exceeded errors
uncompressed images consuming excess storage
AI tags missing silently
evidence: src/services/mediaService.ts handles upload orchestration. supabase/functions/image-upload/ for server processing. Media AI tagging in mediaAITagging.ts.

title: Chat message loss on WebSocket reconnect requires backfill
tip: Supabase realtime postgres_changes does NOT replay events missed during WebSocket disconnection. When the channel status returns to SUBSCRIBED after a drop, fetch messages since the last known server timestamp and merge with client-side deduplication by message ID. Also handle visibilitychange for mobile background/foreground transitions.
applies_when: any realtime subscription where data loss is unacceptable
avoid_when: ephemeral presence indicators where gaps are acceptable
subtasks: track last known server timestamp in ref
fetch gap on SUBSCRIBED status change
deduplicate by message ID on merge
handle visibilitychange event
signals: messages from others missing after connectivity drop
chat appears stale after phone screen-off
no error shown but messages silently lost
evidence: DEBUG_PATTERNS.md: Chat messages lost during websocket reconnect (fixed March 2026). LESSONS.md: Always backfill on realtime channel reconnect.

title: Voice session lifecycle: LiveKit agent deployment + room metadata are prerequisites for voice to work
tip: The voice stack has three components: (1) frontend useLiveKitVoice.ts, (2) livekit-token Supabase edge function, (3) standalone LiveKit agent in agent/. The agent is NOT a Supabase edge function — it must be deployed to LiveKit Cloud separately. Room metadata (tripId, userId, voice) must be set via RoomServiceClient.createRoom(), NOT via AccessToken.roomConfig (which is dead code for metadata). Agent dispatch requires agents:[{agentName:'chravel-voice'}] in createRoom(). Circuit breaker (5 failures in 5 min) prevents infinite reconnect loops. VOICE_RUNTIME flag selects between 'livekit' (default) and 'vertex' (legacy).
subtasks: verify agent deployed to LiveKit Cloud
verify RoomServiceClient.createRoom() sets metadata
verify agent dispatch name matches 'chravel-voice'
validate GOOGLE_API_KEY in agent before model init
check circuit breaker thresholds
signals: Agent did not join within timeout
empty room metadata in agent logs
voice session silently dies
circuit breaker stuck open
evidence: April 2026 forensic audit: (token as any).roomConfig was dead code — AccessToken.toJwt() ignores arbitrary properties. Agent had no Dockerfile or CI pipeline. Fixed by using RoomServiceClient + adding Dockerfile + deploy-agent.yml.

title: Calendar bi-sync must be idempotent — deduplicate by external event ID not insert order
tip: When syncing with Google Calendar, events can arrive multiple times via webhook replay, manual re-sync, or concurrent sync runs. Always use the external event ID (Google Calendar event ID) as the idempotency key. Upsert on external_id rather than insert-and-check. Calendar sync edge function must handle partial failures — some events may sync while others fail, so persist per-event sync status rather than all-or-nothing.
avoid_when: local-only trip events with no external sync
subtasks: verify upsert uses external_id as conflict key
check for duplicate event creation on webhook replay
test partial sync failure recovery
validate trip_event_attendees consistency after sync
signals: duplicate events appearing after re-sync
events disappearing then reappearing
attendee list out of sync with Google Calendar
evidence: PLATFORM_AUDIT_CONSTITUTION.md §4: IDEM-1 identifies missing idempotency keys on create mutations including calendar events. Calendar sync edge function listed in SECURITY_AUDIT_REPORT.md CRIT-02 as using wildcard CORS.

title: Payment splits are state machines — track pending/confirmed/settled per participant with optimistic locking
tip: Payment settlement has a confirmed race condition (RACE-1): two users can simultaneously settle the same balance, creating a double-credit. Use optimistic locking with a version column or SELECT FOR UPDATE on the balance row before settlement. Each settlement must transition through pending→confirmed→settled states per participant. Stripe webhook replay must be idempotent — use webhook event ID as deduplication key. Never trust client-side balance calculations for the settlement amount.
subtasks: verify optimistic locking on settlement mutations
check Stripe webhook idempotency handling
test concurrent settlement scenario
validate balance recalculation after settlement
signals: trip overcredited after concurrent settlements
duplicate Stripe webhook creates double payment record
balance shows negative after settlement
stale balance in settle dialog
evidence: PLATFORM_AUDIT_CONSTITUTION.md §4 RACE-1: Payment Settlement Double-Credit (CRITICAL) — concurrent settles can double-credit. SECURITY_SCALE_AUDIT_2026_03_07.md: webhook idempotency gaps identified.

provenance.task: Memory expansion — Payments & Expense Splitting coverage

title: Media uploads need server-side validation, signed URLs, and cleanup triggers
tip: Never trust client-side MIME type or dimensions for uploaded media. Storage bucket currently uses public URLs with no expiry — anyone with the URL can access private trip photos forever. Switch to signed URLs (1-hour expiry) and set bucket policy to private. Storage quota is advisory-only (UI warns but does not block) — enforce at edge function level. Add CASCADE cleanup triggers so deleting trip_media_index rows also removes storage objects. Rate limit uploads to prevent storage API exhaustion.
avoid_when: reading already-stored media with valid signed URLs
subtasks: verify signed URLs used instead of public URLs
check server-side MIME and size validation
test storage quota enforcement at edge function
verify cleanup trigger on media deletion
check upload rate limiting
signals: private photos accessible via direct URL without auth
storage costs growing without bound from orphaned files
upload succeeds despite quota exceeded
gallery loads slowly from full-resolution images
evidence: PLATFORM_AUDIT_CONSTITUTION.md §7: MEDIA-1 No signed URLs (CRITICAL), MEDIA-2 Storage quota not enforced (HIGH), MEDIA-3 No cleanup on deletion (HIGH), MEDIA-5 No image optimization. SECURITY_SCALE_AUDIT_2026_03_07.md: file upload abuse prevention gaps.

title: Single map instance per page with debounced events — never duplicate MapView
tip: Google Maps JavaScript API is expensive to initialize and memory-heavy. Use one MapView component per page with mode props (view/edit/search) rather than multiple map instances. Debounce high-frequency events (drag, zoom, bounds_changed) at 300ms. Always null-check mapRef.current before operations. Clean up all event listeners in useEffect return. Type all coordinates as { lat: number; lng: number }.
avoid_when: static location display that could use an image instead
subtasks: verify single MapView instance per page
check debounce on drag/zoom/bounds_changed
verify event listener cleanup in useEffect
null-check mapRef.current before operations
signals: multiple map instances rendering on same page
janky map interactions on mobile
memory leak from uncleaned event listeners
TypeError on mapRef.current when map unmounts
evidence: CLAUDE.md §Google Maps Rules: One map instance per page, debounce 300ms, clean up listeners. PLATFORM_AUDIT_CONSTITUTION.md: Maps listed as feature domain.

title: Role propagation must flow DB → RLS → hook → UI — never trust client-side role claims
tip: Permission models differ by trip type (consumer=open, pro=role-based, event=organizer-only). Use a single useMutationPermissions(tripId) hook that resolves trip type and returns flat boolean flags. The guard is client-side UX only — RLS is always authoritative. Never bypass membership validation based on client-side email comparison. The historical SUPER_ADMIN_EMAILS client-side bypass was misleading dead code — RLS via is_super_admin() SQL function was the actual enforcement.
applies_when: adding permission checks to shared hooks
any mutation that checks user role
avoid_when: read-only display where RLS handles filtering
demo mode (uses mock data, not Supabase)
subtasks: verify useMutationPermissions used for role checks
confirm RLS policies match client-side guards
check no client-side email-based admin bypass
test role propagation across consumer/pro/event trip types
signals: permission denied despite appearing authorized in UI
client-side shows admin features but mutations fail
role change not reflected until page refresh
super admin bypass working on client but blocked by RLS
evidence: SECURITY_AUDIT_REPORT.md CRIT-03: Demo account super admin privileges. DEBUG_PATTERNS.md §5: Client-Side Super Admin Bypass (Misleading Dead Code). LESSONS.md: Unified permission guard hook for multi-trip-type codebases.

title: Realtime subscriptions must filter by trip_id — unfiltered channels receive ALL global events
tip: Supabase postgres_changes subscriptions without a filter parameter on tables like message_read_receipts (which lack a trip_id column) receive INSERT events for ALL rows across ALL trips globally. This is invisible at low scale but becomes a bandwidth/CPU crisis at event scale (4000+ members). Either add a filterable column to the table or use client-side filtering with a Set of known IDs. Also: notification fanout is synchronous in the INSERT transaction — a broadcast to 4000 members blocks until all delivery rows are created.
applies_when: event-type trips with large member counts
avoid_when: small consumer trips under 20 members where global events are negligible
subtasks: verify all subscriptions have filter parameter where possible
check tables lacking trip_id column for alternative filtering
test notification fanout under load
validate reconnect correction refetches from DB
signals: excessive bandwidth on idle chat pages
CPU spike when other trips are active
notification delivery blocking INSERT transaction
badge count drift after reconnect
evidence: LESSONS.md: Supabase realtime subscriptions without table-column filters receive ALL events globally. NOTIFICATION_AUDIT.md: Event-scale fanout creates 4000 notification + 12000 delivery rows synchronously. DEBUG_PATTERNS.md: Chat read receipt write amplification.

title: Trip access requires both existence check AND RLS-gated membership — existence does not equal access
tip: Trip existence (row exists in trips table) does not mean the current user has access. All trip data queries must go through RLS which checks trip_members membership. Never validate access by checking if a trip ID exists — always let RLS filter. All IDs must be validated as UUID format and non-null before querying. Auth state must fully resolve before data fetch guards evaluate — otherwise stale auth triggers false Not Found.
avoid_when: public trip preview pages that intentionally show limited data without membership
subtasks: verify RLS policy checks trip_members for all trip-scoped tables
confirm UUID format validation on trip_id parameters
check auth hydration completes before trip access guard
test accessing trip URL without membership
signals: trip data visible to non-members
Trip Not Found for valid trip when auth is slow
type error from malformed trip_id parameter
data leak via direct Supabase REST API call with anon key
evidence: CLAUDE.md §Security Gate: Trip existence ≠ trip access. Auth state must resolve before data fetch. SECURITY_AUDIT_REPORT.md: trip ID validation issues identified. PLATFORM_AUDIT_CONSTITUTION.md §3: RLS policies check membership before data access.

title: Gmail and PDF import failures must persist partial state — never silently drop parsed items on retry
tip: When Gmail import or PDF parsing fails mid-stream, the already-parsed items must be persisted in a partial terminal state (e.g., run_status=partial_success with item-level status tracking). Never discard successfully parsed items because a later item failed. On retry, use the external message ID or file hash as idempotency key to skip already-ingested items. The import UI must surface partial counts (e.g., '3 of 5 items imported — 2 failed') rather than a generic success/failure toast.
subtasks: verify run-level and item-level status tracking
check idempotency key prevents re-import of same item
test partial failure shows accurate counts in UI
validate retry skips already-ingested items
signals: success toast but fewer items than expected
duplicate items after retry
generic error toast hiding partial success
cannot determine which items were imported from which source
evidence: agent_memory.jsonl entry 4: Treat imports as state machines. TEST_GAPS.md: Integrations platform replay/idempotency testing identified as high-priority gap.

title: Concierge tool declarations must be a single source of truth — unified in toolRegistry.ts
tip: The AI Concierge now has 75 unified tools in _shared/concierge/toolRegistry.ts. Both text and voice paths import from this single registry. All 75 tools have matching implementations in functionExecutor.ts. When adding new tools, update all 5 locations: toolRegistry.ts (declaration + QUERY_CLASS_TOOLS + voice override), agent/src/tools.ts (ToolDefinition), functionExecutor.ts (case block). For pending-buffer write tools, also update usePendingActions.ts (confirm handler) and PendingActionCard.tsx (TOOL_CONFIG).
subtasks: add new tool schema to toolRegistry.ts
add matching case in functionExecutor.ts
assign tool to correct query classes in registry
test tool via both text and voice paths
signals: voice tool call returns error
tool works in text but fails in voice
new tool added but not in registry
evidence: Refactored in commit eb4094b: 38 tools unified via toolRegistry.ts. AUDIT_CONCIERGE_ARCHITECTURE_2026_03_18.md updated with post-refactor status.

title: Concierge tools are conditionally loaded by query class via queryClassifier.ts
tip: 75 tools are available but now conditionally loaded per query class via queryClassifier.ts + toolRegistry.ts. The query classifier maps 18 query classes (weather, restaurant, calendar, payment, task, navigation, etc.) to relevant tool subsets. A weather question sees only searchWeb. Restaurant recommendations see searchPlaces, getPlaceDetails, savePlace, emitReservationDraft. This saves ~1500-2000 tokens on simple queries. When adding new tools, assign them to the correct query classes in toolRegistry.ts. STATUS: IMPLEMENTED. 75 tools across 18+ query classes. trip_summary is catch-all (all tools). Universal tools (verify_artifact, explainPermission, convertCurrency) always loaded.
avoid_when: queries that genuinely need multi-domain tools (classifier has fallback_full class)
subtasks: assign new tools to correct query classes
verify fallback_full class handles ambiguous queries
measure token savings with PostHog
test query classifier edge cases
signals: high prompt_tokens on simple queries (regression)
model selecting irrelevant tools
new tool not appearing for relevant queries
evidence: Implemented in commit eb4094b: queryClassifier.ts + toolRegistry.ts. AUDIT_CONCIERGE_ARCHITECTURE_2026_03_18.md §4-6 updated with post-refactor status.

title: Pending action confirm handler must have a case for every pending-buffer write tool
tip: usePendingActions.ts has a switch on tool_name that executes the actual DB write when the user confirms a pending action. It only had 3 cases (createTask, createPoll, addToCalendar) but 8+ tools write to trip_pending_actions. Any tool_name not in the switch throws 'Unknown tool: X' silently — the confirm button appears to work but no data is written. Fix: add an explicit case for every pending-buffer write tool. For tools where the pending_action IS the record (addReminder, setTripBudget), just break — no secondary write needed.
applies_when: adding new pending-buffer write tools to the AI concierge
debugging confirm card that appears to work but produces no data
any tool that inserts to trip_pending_actions
avoid_when: direct-execution write tools (updateCalendarEvent, deleteTask, settleExpense etc.) — these never hit usePendingActions.ts
signals: confirm button dismisses the card but no task/event/expense appears
Unknown tool: X error in console on confirm
pending action marked confirmed but no DB row created
evidence: Discovered during 74-tool expansion (April 2026): addReminder and setTripBudget had been broken since addition. All new pending-buffer tools added in this session required matching confirm handlers in usePendingActions.ts.

provenance.task: 74→75 tool expansion, pending actions infrastructure fix

title: Concierge tool additions require 5-file sync — miss one and the tool silently fails
tip: Adding a new concierge tool requires updating exactly 5 locations. (1) toolRegistry.ts: declaration + QUERY_CLASS_TOOLS entry + voice override. (2) agent/src/tools.ts: ToolDefinition with Zod schema + add to ALL_TOOLS array. (3) functionExecutor.ts: switch case with actual logic. (4) usePendingActions.ts: confirm handler IF the tool uses insertPendingAction. (5) PendingActionCard.tsx: TOOL_CONFIG entry + title/detail helpers IF the tool uses insertPendingAction. Skipping (4)/(5) for pending-buffer tools means the confirm step throws 'Unknown tool'. Skipping (2) means the LiveKit voice agent can't call the tool. The 3-file assumption (registry + agent + executor) is incomplete for write tools.
applies_when: adding any new AI concierge tool
debugging tool that works in text but not voice, or confirms silently
avoid_when: read-only tools that use callEdgeFunction — those only need the 3 core files
signals: tool callable from text but not voice
confirm card appears but data not created
Unknown tool error in frontend console
evidence: Pattern established across 38→42→60→74→75 tool expansions in April 2026. Direct-execution write tools (moveCalendarEvent, closePoll etc.) need only 3 files; pending-buffer tools need all 5.

title: Mock-ID tier gate disables consumer-only features for all real trips
tip: isConsumerTrip(tripId) in src/utils/tripTierDetector.ts only returns true for hardcoded mock IDs '1'..'12'. Any production gate written as `if (isConsumerTrip(id))` is dead in production for every UUID trip. Use the new useTripType(tripId) hook (which queries trips.trip_type with a 5-minute cache) for production gates; reserve isConsumerTrip for demo content rendering only. For non-React contexts, query trips.trip_type directly and cache.
applies_when: gating chat/event-log/notification behavior by trip tier
auditing why a feature is invisible in production despite working UI/persistence
avoid_when: test fixtures that genuinely use mock IDs
signals: UI toggle persists state but no observable behavior change in production
feature works on demo trip ID 1 but not on real UUID trip
EventLog/EventLogDrawer renders empty for real trips
evidence: Discovered April 2026 inline-chat-activity audit: settings UI, persistence, RLS, and render filter were all correct; the entire feature was disabled in production by isConsumerTrip mock-ID gate. Fixed in src/hooks/useTripType.ts + call-site swaps in TripChat.tsx, EventLogDrawer.tsx, systemMessageService.ts.

provenance.task: Inline chat activity updates audit + fix

title: Stream custom message fields require explicit forwarding through both adapter paths
tip: Adding a new custom field to Stream messages (e.g. system_event_type, system_payload) requires updating BOTH the typed adapter (src/services/stream/adapters/mappers/messageMapper.ts: streamMessageToChravel + chravelMessageToStreamPayload + ChrravelChatMessage typedef) AND the in-component mapping in src/features/chat/components/TripChat.tsx (liveFormattedMessages map). Render-side filters silently degrade to no-op if the field is undefined at the call site. Add a round-trip mapper test to force both paths through the same gate.
applies_when: any new render-side dependency on Stream custom data
signals: filter or render decision degrades to default ('show everything') in production
(message as any).newField is undefined in MessageItem despite being set on writer side
mapper test absent for new field
evidence: Discovered April 2026 chat activity audit: system_event_type was set by writer but dropped in adapter+TripChat mapping, so shouldShowSystemMessage(undefined) always returned true. Fixed by passthrough in messageMapper.ts + TripChat.tsx + new test messageMapper.systemEvent.test.ts.

provenance.task: Inline chat activity updates audit + fix

title: System messages route through Stream channel.sendMessage with silent + skip_push
tip: Inline activity updates (member joined, poll closed, basecamp moved, etc.) are emitted into the trip's Stream channel as message_type='system' with a structured system_event_type custom field. Use channel.sendMessage(payload, { skip_push: true }) and set silent: true on the payload so Stream does not deliver a push notification — the existing send_notification Supabase pipeline remains the single source of truth for alerts. Gate emission to consumer trips only via systemMessageService.getTripType(tripId), which caches trips.trip_type for 5 minutes. Pro/event trips skip emission to avoid roster spam.
applies_when: wiring a new domain action (poll close, task complete, etc.) to inline activity updates
avoid_when: actions where push notification is the primary signal (use send_notification edge function instead)
signals: activity action triggers a duplicate push notification
system message appears with user_id of acting user (correct — Stream auths sender as the acting user)
no system message appears in chat after consumer-trip action
evidence: Implemented April 2026 chat activity fix: src/services/systemMessageService.ts now calls getStreamClient().channel(CHANNEL_TYPE_TRIP, tripChannelId(tripId)).sendMessage({...system fields, silent: true}, { skip_push: true }). Test coverage in src/services/__tests__/systemMessageService.stream.test.ts.

provenance.task: Inline chat activity updates audit + fix

title: Adjacent issues are fixed in-branch or captured as a Follow-Up Issue Plan — never deferred with vague language
tip: When you discover a related defect, fragile mapping, blocked hook, weak RLS policy, dead code, missing migration, missing test, or regression risk while doing other work, you must do ONE of: (1) fix it in the current branch if it is directly connected to the bug, affects correctness, creates duplicate logic, or is needed for feature reliability, OR (2) produce a paste-ready Follow-Up Issue Plan with all 9 fields filled in (Title, Why this matters, Files likely involved, Current risk, Recommended fix, Acceptance criteria, Test plan, Rollback plan, Launch-blocking?). The phrases 'out of scope', 'future cleanup PR', 'temporary duplication', 'known tech debt', 'could be addressed later', and 'not addressed in this branch' are banned. Critical-path work (auth, chat, media, payments, invites, mobile wrapper) gets a reliability-beats-scope override: if the feature is still fragile after the fix, say so and propose the next fix in the same response. Every coding response ends with a 7-section footer: Fixed now / Discovered / Intentionally deferred / Why deferral was necessary / Paste-ready follow-up prompt / Validation completed / Remaining launch blockers.
applies_when: any coding task that touches multiple modules or surfaces
bug fixes where adjacent issues are visible
any task on auth, chat, media, payments, invites, or mobile critical paths
avoid_when: read-only research with no code changes (footer still applies but Fixed now is empty)
signals: agent response uses banned phrases like 'out of scope' or 'known tech debt'
PR description says 'will address later' without an issue link
duplicate logic introduced without all four carve-out conditions met
response missing the 7-section footer
evidence: Defined in DEFERRAL_DISCIPLINE.md; surfaced in CLAUDE.md § DEFERRAL DISCIPLINE and AGENTS.md § NON-NEGOTIABLES.

provenance.task: Codify deferral discipline as a project-wide rule

title: Remove a value from a shared CHECK constraint by widening, not tightening
tip: When deleting one value from an enum-style CHECK (col IN (...)) on a SHARED table, leave the constraint permissive. Historical rows may still carry the value; tightening requires purging/migrating them and risks failing on production data for zero functional gain. Stop PRODUCING the value at its source (the fan-out trigger / dispatcher) instead, and mark any already-queued rows as skipped to preserve audit history.
applies_when: dropping one value from a shared status/type/channel CHECK
deprecating an enum member on a table with historical rows
avoid_when: the column is brand new with no rows
the value was never written to production
signals: migration ALTERs a CHECK to remove a value
DROP COLUMN on a shared multi-tenant table
queued rows for a removed channel could get stuck
evidence: 20260604120000_remove_sms_notifications.sql recreated queue_notification_deliveries() to emit push+email only, marked queued channel='sms' rows skipped, and intentionally left notification_deliveries.channel / notification_logs.type CHECKs widened.

title: Surgically removing a channel woven into a shared dispatcher: producer-first, then drop in dependency order
tip: To remove a delivery channel from a shared notification stack: (1) CREATE OR REPLACE the fan-out trigger function to stop emitting the channel; (2) neutralize already-queued rows (status='skipped'); (3) drop dependent trigger -> function -> table -> columns in dependency order; (4) CREATE OR REPLACE any shared function that referenced the dropped columns so it is column-safe (plpgsql resolves RECORD field access at runtime, so an unreached CASE arm won't fail at drop time, but recreate it anyway to kill the latent failure). Also trace function OVERLOADS: should_send_notification had a 2-arg and a 3-arg version; only the 3-arg survived and referenced the dropped columns.
applies_when: removing SMS/email/push or any channel from notification dispatch
dropping columns referenced by SECURITY DEFINER plpgsql functions
deprecating a feature whose schema is shared with live features
avoid_when: the schema object is feature-isolated with no shared table or function
signals: a shared trigger inserts per-channel rows
a function uses SELECT * INTO RECORD then references soon-to-be-dropped fields
multiple overloads of the same function name exist
evidence: Twilio/SMS removal: recreated queue_notification_deliveries() and should_send_notification() column-safe before dropping notification_preferences.sms_* columns, sms_opt_in, and the SMS functions.

title: A file named after the feature you're removing is often shared with a live path — grep consumers first
tip: Before deleting a file whose name matches the feature being removed, grep every importer. During LiveKit removal, _shared/vertexAuth.ts (used by concierge-tts + fcmV1 push), voiceToolDeclarations.ts (text concierge), and a generic formatTimeForTimezone helper living inside smsTemplates.ts (used by event-reminders push) all looked removable by name but were shared. Relocate genuinely-shared helpers into a neutral module and repoint importers rather than deleting them with the feature.
applies_when: any infra cleanup that deletes files by name match
avoid_when: grep confirms the file has zero importers outside the removed feature
signals: a _shared util sits in a feature-named file
a 'voice' or 'sms' helper is imported by a push/email/text path
deleting a file breaks an unrelated function's build
evidence: formatTimeForTimezone relocated from smsTemplates.ts to notificationUtils.ts and event-reminders repointed; vertexAuth/voiceToolDeclarations/voiceProductPath kept because text concierge + push depend on them.

title: Advertised limits need an enforcement call-site audit, not a config read
tip: Chravel billing limits live in config (FEATURE_LIMITS, featureTiers) but several shipped with ZERO enforcement call sites (payment splits 3/trip, event attendee caps 100/200) while others were enforced twice (trip cap: client + edge). Before citing or building on a limit, grep for consumers of the limit constant; a limit with no call sites is marketing copy, not a gate — and it's also a wasted upgrade trigger.
applies_when: adding/auditing tier limits, paywall work, pricing copy changes, synthetic QA of monetization

title: Settlement-style crediting transitions must be single-RPC with row lock + status guard
tip: payment_splits settlement raced (read-then-write double-credit). Fix pattern now in place: settle_payment_split / settle_payment_splits_for_debtor / unsettle_payment_split RPCs lock the parent trip_payment_messages row, guard on is_settled, check caller is debtor-or-creator, and report ALREADY_SETTLED as idempotent success. Client rule (pinned by paymentSettlement.atomic.test.ts): crediting writes never touch payment_splits directly; only the non-crediting pending mark may.
applies_when: payment settlement changes, any one-way state transition that credits money or consumes quota, retry/double-click bug reports

title: Verify a migration actually applied before building on its objects
tip: The 'canonical' notification fanout migration (20260512160000) referenced tables that don't exist (trip_broadcasts, calendar_events) so it never applied to prod — its functions/columns are absent from generated types, and a later 'fix' migration built on top without noticing. Signal: objects a migration creates are missing from src/integrations/supabase/types.ts. Corrective migrations must be self-contained (recreate prerequisites idempotently) and DROP TRIGGER IF EXISTS still errors when the TABLE is missing — guard with information_schema checks.
applies_when: writing migrations that ALTER/REPLACE objects from earlier migrations, debugging triggers that never fire, schema-drift investigations

title: Vite manualChunks object form strands the preload helper — every route then downloads that chunk at boot
tip: With object-form manualChunks, Vite's dynamic-import preload helper (vite/preload-helper) is unassigned and Rollup colocated it inside the 'pdf' chunk; since every lazy chunk imports the helper, all ~25 routes statically pulled 624KB of jspdf/html2canvas at boot (entry preload list is the proof: grep dist for m.f=[...] and check which chunk exports `_`). Fix: function-form manualChunks that pins id.includes('vite/preload-helper') to an always-loaded vendor chunk. Identical before/after chunk sizes confirm vendor membership is otherwise unchanged.
applies_when: entry chunk or boot path unexpectedly large, manualChunks changes, bundle audits where a lazy-only library appears in every page's preload list

title: Reading the Supabase session from storage must use the client's configured storageKey, never the sb-* default
tip: The supabase client sets storageKey 'chravel-auth-session' (now exported as SUPABASE_AUTH_STORAGE_KEY from integrations/supabase/config.ts). A feature that scanned for default sb-*-auth-token keys (query-cache persister owner stamp) matched nothing and was silently inert — all its fail-closed paths hid the breakage; worse, stale legacy sb-* residue from old builds could satisfy the scan with the WRONG user. Always import the constant; a round-trip test seeded under the real key is the repro that catches this.
applies_when: any boot-time auth heuristic or persistence layer reading localStorage, parsing Supabase sessions outside the client, features that mysteriously never activate in prod

title: Adding TTL stamps to a Zustand store requires stamp hygiene in every other setter plus a write-generation guard
tip: entitlementsStore TTL (ownerUserId+lastSyncedAt) had two bugs found in review: (1) setDemoMode(true) overwrote plan state but left old stamps, so the non-forced refresh after demo exit was TTL-skipped and the demo 'frequent-chraveler' plan survived up to 5 min — override setters must clear the stamps they invalidate; (2) a slow in-flight refresh resolving after clear()/sign-out or after a forced refresh overwrote newer state — guard every async write with a module-level generation counter bumped by refresh starts AND clear().
applies_when: adding caching/TTL to a shared store, stores with demo/admin override setters, sign-out clearing logic, post-purchase force-refresh paths

title: A Tailwind class that 'does nothing' may be undefined — grep the config before styling around it
tip: glass-orange/glass-yellow/glass-crimson/glass-blue were referenced in 31 files (auth submit buttons, upgrade-modal toggles, org tab accents) but defined nowhere, so Tailwind emitted no CSS for months — elements rendered with no background/focus state. Signal: a styled element looks unstyled and its class name isn't in tailwind.config.ts or index.css. Verify fixes at the artifact level: grep the emitted dist/assets/*.css for the selector (mind backslash escaping like scale-\[0\.98\]). Guard test: src/__tests__/no-dead-accent-classes.test.ts.

title: Dynamic Tailwind class interpolation never generates CSS — provide literal class strings
tip: TripVariantContext supplied bare token names consumed as `text-${accentColors.primary}` / `from-${...}/20` — Tailwind's scanner only sees literal strings, so none of these classes were generated even when the token was valid. Replace interpolation with full literal classes at the call site (or safelist). Same trap: classes only mentioned in comments DO get scanned, and `@apply some-class` copies along every rule targeting that class, including `.light .some-class` remaps (useful: .modal-backdrop inherited light-mode behavior for free).

title: Custom CSS 'background:' shorthand silently wipes Tailwind bg-* utilities on the same element
tip: skeleton-shimmer used the background shorthand for its gradient; combined with bg-muted/50 on ui/skeleton.tsx the shorthand reset background-color (cascade order put the custom utility later), leaving skeletons nearly invisible in light mode. Use background-image + background-size in custom utility/component classes so utility background-color survives. Also: borders added to fixed-height primitives (TabsList h-10) grow content past the box — prefer ring/shadow (box-shadow) for active edges, zero geometry impact.

title: Homepage media must be repo-committed static files, never sandbox asset-store URLs
tip: A previous agent rendered the hero demo video and uploaded it to its sandbox asset proxy (/__l5e/assets-v1/...) via src/assets/*.asset.json pointer files. Those URLs only resolve inside that agent's container, so production showed a broken video AND broken poster (the fallback img used the same dead URL). Marketing media consumed by the homepage must live in public/ (served by Vercel with correct MIME) and be committed; verify with curl against vite preview before shipping.
subtasks: grep src/ for asset.json and __l5e references
curl the asset URL on the built preview server
confirm fallback poster uses a working URL
signals: video element renders alt text/broken image in prod
asset URL starts with /__l5e/ or another sandbox path
poster 404s while file exists in repo
evidence: HeroSection.tsx imported chravel-homepage-demo-60.mp4.asset.json pointing at /__l5e/assets-v1/ce56c258.../chravel-homepage-demo-60.mp4 — 404 on Vercel and local preview; fixed by committing public/videos/chravel-homepage-demo-60.mp4 (branch claude/chravel-homepage-demo-video-4cxuni).

title: Playwright-bundled Chromium cannot decode H.264 — a failing <video> in sandbox tests is not a prod failure
tip: In this remote environment (chromium at /opt/pw-browsers/chromium-1194), canPlayType('video/mp4; codecs=avc1...') returns '' and an mp4 <video> fires error code 4 (DEMUXER_ERROR_NO_SUPPORTED_STREAMS). Before diagnosing the encode, check canPlayType in the same browser. Validate mp4s structurally instead (moov-before-mdat for faststart, mvhd duration, avc1 box) and rely on the poster-fallback path, which IS testable headlessly. Note the playwright ffmpeg binary (/opt/pw-browsers/ffmpeg-1011) also cannot demux mp4.
subtasks: check canPlayType before trusting onError
parse mp4 boxes in python for duration/codec/faststart
test the poster/fallback branch headlessly
signals: MEDIA_ERR code 4 with HTTP 200 asset
DEMUXER_ERROR_NO_SUPPORTED_STREAMS in v.error.message
evidence: Homepage demo validation: HTTP 200 + valid 60s avc1 mp4, but playwright chromium reported canH264='' and errored; fallback img rendered correctly proving the markup logic.

title: Remotion renders in this sandbox: glibc compositor, local fonts, cert-ignore
tip: Three environment traps for remotion/ renders: (1) package.json pinned @remotion/compositor-linux-x64-musl which hard-fails npm install on this glibc container — let @remotion/renderer's optionalDependencies pick the right compositor; (2) fonts.gstatic.com is TLS-resigned by the egress proxy so @remotion/google-fonts loadFont cancels renders with NetworkError — use @remotion/fonts + staticFile woff2 (vendored in remotion/public/fonts, copied from public/fonts) for new compositions and pass ignoreCertificateErrors for legacy ones; (3) point browserExecutable at /opt/pw-browsers/chromium-1194/chrome-linux/chrome with chromeMode chrome-for-testing.
avoid_when: local dev machines with normal network (google-fonts works there)
subtasks: npm install in remotion/ and check compositor-linux-x64-gnu landed
render one still before the full 1800-frame render
grep render logs for ERR_CERT_AUTHORITY_INVALID
signals: npm error notsup Valid libc: musl
NetworkError: A network error occurred during still/render
fonts.gstatic.com ERR_CERT_AUTHORITY_INVALID in verbose logs
evidence: remotion install failed (musl pin) then HomepageProductDemo60 stills failed with NetworkError until fonts went local (remotion/src/fonts.ts) and --ignore-certificate-errors was added; full 1800-frame render then succeeded.

title: Deferral-table follow-ups from a prior session may name artifacts that don't exist on the current branch — verify each premise before implementing
tip: A pasted perf follow-up table referenced a query persister ('chravel-query-cache'), 'bootAuthMarker.ts', and an entitlements TTL that existed in no branch; the real codebase analogues had different names (bootstrapShell.ts, ad-hoc localStorage check in main.tsx) or didn't exist at all. Grep for each named artifact first; map each item to what actually exists, and report not-applicable items honestly instead of inventing work. Bonus: the 'consolidate useBilling polling' item dissolved entirely once grep showed useBilling/useEntitlements had zero consumers — deleting dead code WAS the consolidation (knip_output.txt already flagged them).
applies_to: acting on follow-up lists or audit tables pasted from another session

title: Skeleton consolidation: per-element shimmer on large grids regresses the perf it's meant to signal — use one container animation past ~10 cells
tip: ui/skeleton.tsx's skeleton-shimmer is a per-element background-position animation; converting CalendarSkeleton's 35-cell grid to 35 <Skeleton> elements meant 35 concurrent paint-driving animations on low-end mobile where one container animate-pulse did the job before. Pattern: individual <Skeleton> for small counts; for big grids/lists keep cells as static bg-muted/50 divs inside one animate-pulse container. Also: hardcoded bg-white/10 overrides on <Skeleton> defeat token propagation — strip them with a guarded sed (/<Skeleton/s/ bg-white\/10//g) and check ALL org-suite files (OrganizationDashboard, OrganizationsHub, SeatManagement shared the idiom).

title: Never pass localStorage/sessionStorage as function arguments — the global's getter itself throws in cookie-blocked browsers
tip: main.tsx passed bare `localStorage` to hasAuthStorageMarker() at module scope; in cookie-blocked Chrome the window.localStorage GETTER throws SecurityError, so the throw happens at the call site outside any try/catch — black-screening the app before boot. Same for telemetry init's raw localStorage.getItem('TRIPS_DEMO_MODE') (a throw there permanently aborted telemetry init). Use src/utils/safeStorage.ts kind-based accessors (safeGetItem('local', key)) which resolve the Storage object INSIDE try/catch; an API like safeGetItem(storage, key) would re-create the bug.
applies_to: any new storage helper API design
telemetry or feature-detection code running before error boundaries exist

title: Native Sign in with Apple uses id-token, not browser OAuth, on iOS/iPad
tip: When the chravel-mobile shell exposes window.ChravelNative.signInWithApple() (native ASAuthorization returning { identityToken, rawNonce, authorizationCode }), authenticate via supabase.auth.signInWithIdToken({ provider:'apple', token: identityToken, nonce: rawNonce }) and pass the RAW unhashed nonce because the shell sends SHA256(nonce) to Apple. This skips the SFSafariViewController/Universal-Link round-trip that strands iPad users (App Review 2.1(a)). Keep the web signInWithOAuth flow as a byte-for-byte fallback: the helper returns { handled:false } when the bridge is absent, throws, or returns an incomplete credential.
subtasks: check window.ChravelNative.signInWithApple via getNativeAppleSignIn
call signInWithIdToken with the raw nonce
fall back to signInWithOAuth on handled:false
forward authorizationCode for revocation capture
signals: Apple login does not advance after Face/Touch ID
user stranded in Safari after authenticating
native shell present but OAuth opens external browser
evidence: src/utils/nativeAppleSignIn.ts (attemptNativeAppleSignIn) + src/hooks/useAuth.tsx signInWithApple, merged in PR #746. Bridge contract documented in src/utils/nativeBridge.ts.

provenance.task: App Store 2.1(a) Apple Sign In iPad fix

title: App Store native-shell rejections are fixed in chravel-mobile, not chravel-web
tip: chravel-web is only the WebView payload. The iOS binary, Info.plist (UIBackgroundModes, e.g. background-audio under Guideline 2.5.4), entitlements, and Associated Domains live in the separate chravel-mobile Expo/EAS repo. chravel-web owns only the AASA file (public/.well-known/apple-app-site-association), the Supabase OAuth flow, and the window.ChravelNative bridge contract (src/utils/nativeBridge.ts). Before attempting an App Store rejection fix in chravel-web, confirm which repo owns the artifact; most native-config rejections cannot be resolved from chravel-web and need a chravel-mobile change plus an App Store Connect reply.
applies_when: deciding which repo a mobile fix belongs in
avoid_when: Supabase-side or AASA-file changes (these ARE in chravel-web)
subtasks: confirm package.json has no @capacitor/* or @expo/* deps
check if the artifact is app.json/Info.plist (chravel-mobile) vs AASA/OAuth (chravel-web)
produce a paste-ready chravel-mobile prompt + App Store Connect reply for parts web cannot fix
signals: rejection cites UIBackgroundModes / background audio
rejection cites Associated Domains / deep-link callback
a tool assumes Xcode/Capacitor paths that do not exist in this repo
evidence: June 2026 App Store rejection (2.5.4 background audio + 2.1(a) Apple Sign In). APP_STORE_READINESS_AUDIT.md documents the repo split; PR #746 shipped only the web half (native id-token path).

title: Red 'Unit Tests (Vitest)' aggregate is often a forks-worker teardown flake; format:check fails repo-wide
tip: Two CI gotchas that block PRs without a real code defect. (1) 'Unit Tests (Vitest)' is an aggregate that rolls up the shard matrix (result=failure -> exit 1); open the specific shard log. '[vitest-pool]: Timeout terminating forks worker for <file>' printed AFTER the coverage table means the tests passed and the pool failed to tear down in time, a flake a re-run clears. The Codecov 'Token required - not valid tokenless upload' line is a separate non-failing if:always() step. (2) Static Checks runs prettier --check over the whole src/ tree, so a feature PR inherits any pre-existing unformatted files from main (verify with git diff origin/main). Fix with prettier --write using the repo's pinned version, as a separate chore: commit; expect it to widen Cross-PR File Overlap.
applies_when: PR CI red on Unit Tests (Vitest) or a single shard
CI failure on files the PR did not touch
avoid_when: a shard log shows an actual assertion failure (real bug)
format:check fails only on files the PR edited
subtasks: read the failing shard log, not just the aggregate
confirm the coverage table printed before the worker-timeout line
git diff origin/main to prove unformatted files are pre-existing
prettier --write then npm run format:check to verify clean
re-run to clear the shard flake
signals: aggregate says one or more Vitest shards failed or were cancelled
Timeout terminating forks worker
Code style issues found in N files for files outside the diff
evidence: PR #746: shard 4 flaked on AIConciergeChat.test.tsx (teardown timeout, passed on re-run); Static Checks failed on 21 files byte-identical to main, fixed in a separate chore commit.

provenance.task: PR #746 CI triage (Static Checks + Unit Tests shard)
errors: One or more Vitest shards failed or were cancelled
Code style issues found in 21 files

title: iOS WKWebView: scrolled-in tabs/children stop receiving taps in a momentum + scroll-snap row inside a position:fixed shell
tip: On iOS WKWebView (Capacitor/TestFlight), a horizontally-scrolling row with `scroll-snap-type: x mandatory` plus `-webkit-overflow-scrolling: touch`, nested inside a `position: fixed` ancestor, promotes its own compositor layer whose hit-test rects are NOT updated after a horizontal scroll. Children visible at scroll position 0 keep working; children scrolled into view silently stop receiving taps (tap never fires, so any active-state highlight never changes) even though the row still scrolls. The symptom partitions by horizontal position, which masquerades as 'feature X breaks everything after it'. Worse when the snap has no real targets (e.g. a child `scroll-snap-align-start` class that doesn't exist — the real Tailwind class is `snap-start`). Fix: drop `scroll-snap-type` + `-webkit-overflow-scrolling: touch` from the row, add `touch-action: manipulation`, remove dead snap classes. Plain overflow scrolling hit-tests correctly.
applies_when: mobile tab/chip row taps dead on iOS/TestFlight but fine on web
taps work for leftmost items only / break after horizontal scroll
tab button does not highlight on tap (activeTab never changes)
horizontally-scrollable row nested in a position:fixed mobile shell
avoid_when: the row is not horizontally scrollable
the failure reproduces in jsdom (then it is a logic bug, not hit-testing)
subtasks: check whether the failing items are the ones requiring horizontal scroll, not a specific feature
grep the row container style for scrollSnapType / WebkitOverflowScrolling
verify any scroll-snap-align child class actually exists in Tailwind/CSS
confirm the ancestor shell is position:fixed (recent keyboard-pinning work is a common cause)
remove momentum+snap, add touch-action: manipulation, retest on device
signals: bug only on iOS WKWebView / Capacitor / TestFlight, not web
row still scrolls but scrolled-in items ignore taps
no active highlight change on tap
scroll-snap-type: x mandatory + -webkit-overflow-scrolling: touch on the scroller
evidence: MobileTripTabs: Chat/Calendar/Concierge (visible at rest) switched fine; Media/Payments/Places/Polls/Tasks (scrolled-in) ignored taps on TestFlight. Shell became position:fixed in 'lock trip chat shell to viewport'; tab row had scrollSnapType:'x mandatory' + WebkitOverflowScrolling:'touch' and a non-existent scroll-snap-align-start class. Removing momentum+snap and adding touch-action:manipulation restored taps.

provenance.task: TestFlight: trip tabs not switching after Concierge
errors: scrolled-in tabs do not respond to taps on iOS
